actionable_feedback: Open attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] in a spreadsheet-capable environment and confirm that calculation cells (the 'Calc' rows) contain formulas (not hard-coded numeric results). If any formula cells are values, restore the formulas.

Expose formula text for auditor consumption: add an unprotected 'Formulas' sheet or export a plain-text snippet (e.g., calc_formulas.txt or CSV) showing the formulas for the cited calc ranges (e.g., Calc!A4:D6 or actual ranges used).

In expert_memo.md, add exact spreadsheet cell range references for the per-record calc rows (e.g., 'Calc!A4:D6' or the precise cells used for exponent, factor, and lethality) so verifiers can locate the formulas quickly.

Document the numeric tolerance used for automated numeric recomputation checks in the memo (e.g., absolute tolerance 1e-6 or relative tolerance 1e-9) so future verifiers know acceptable rounding differences.

Include one brief example showing how a percent-change remediation option was applied to a sample cell (numeric example using the multiplicative interpretation) to remove ambiguity for downstream readers.
Add a one-line per-scenario arithmetic note (or a formula row) in the workbook or memo showing how scenario combination costs were computed to speed budget checks.
Ensure calculation sheets are not protected/hidden; if protection is required, supply an unlocked copy or a textual export of formulas for audit.
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: high quality and contains row-level recomputation; add explicit cell-range pointers and numeric tolerance to improve machine/verifier ergonomics.

thermal_lethality_4.xlsx: numeric results appear consistent with memo but the environment could not parse the workbook to confirm formulas; please expose formulas in the revised package (text export or unprotected sheet).
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are complete and numeric recomputation matches the memo and actual_outputs; final_score 1.0 is appropriate. The sole outstanding verifier concern is that the .xlsx workbook could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so the presence of live formulas in calculation cells could not be confirmed automatically. Recommend a small set of auditability-focused fixes to eliminate this manual-verifier step and speed future automated checks.
